Node.help: Configures Ethernet interfaces on Ethernet ports.

PropertyString.obsolete: MacRange list must be used instead. Represents mac for the first element in MacRange list
PropertyString.name: mac
PropertyString.description: base value used when the plugin creates a mac addresses.
PropertyString.help: When a range of multiple macs are created with this plugin, this value is the base value used to create the addresses in the range, this property is used in conjunction with 'incrementBy' as an incrementor.

PropertyString.obsolete: MacRange list must be used instead. Represents incrementBy for the first element in MacRange list
PropertyString.name: incrementBy
PropertyString.description: Incrementor used when the plugin creates a range of macs.
PropertyString.help: When a range of multiple macs are created with this plugin, this value is the incrementor used to create the addresses in the range, this property is used in conjunction with 'mac' as a base value.

PropertyNodeList.name: macRangeList
PropertyNodeList.help: The list of MAC ranges. See MacRange for more details.

PropertyNodeList.name: vlanRangeList
PropertyNodeList.help: The list of vlan ranges. See VlanIdRange for more details.

PropertyBoolean.name: enabled
PropertyBoolean.description: True enables the plugin, false disables the plugin.

PropertyBoolean.name: autoNegotiate
PropertyBoolean.help: If is set then autonegotiation is activated. The purpose of autonegotiation is to find a way for two NICs that share a link to communicate with each other, regardless of whether they both implemented the same Ethernet version or option set.

PropertyString.name: speed
PropertyString.help: The communication speed at witch the physical layer will be set.
